FR Y-9C.name: Consolidated Financial Statements for Holding Companies
FR Y-9C.filer: Top-tier BHCs, SLHCs, IHCs and SHCs with total consolidated assets of $3 billion or more

Charge-Offs and Recoveries on Loans and Leases

FR Y-9LP.name: Parent Company Only Financial Statements for Large Holding Companies
FR Y-9LP.filer: Parent of each top-tier holding company that files the FR Y-9C (one per Y-9C filer per tier)

schedules.title: Investments in Subsidiaries and Associated Companies

FR Y-9SP.name: Parent Company Only Financial Statements for Small Holding Companies
FR Y-9SP.filer: Top-tier domestic BHCs, SLHCs and SHCs with total consolidated assets of less than $3 billion

FR Y-9ES.name: Financial Statements for Employee Stock Ownership Plan Holding Companies
FR Y-9ES.filer: All ESOP bank holding companies and ESOP savings-and-loan holding companies

schedules.title: Statement of Changes in Net Assets Available for Benefits
Statement of Net Assets Available for Benefits

FR Y-9CS.name: Supplement to the Consolidated Financial Statements for Holding Companies
FR Y-9CS.filer: Domestic BHCs, SLHCs, IHCs and SHCs filing the FR Y-9C or FR Y-9SP, as designated when activated

FFIEC 031.name: Consolidated Reports of Condition and Income (banks with domestic and foreign offices)
FFIEC 031.filer: Banks of any size with a foreign office; also domestic-only advanced-approaches banks or banks with total assets of $100 billion or more

FFIEC 041.name: Consolidated Reports of Condition and Income (banks with domestic offices only)
FFIEC 041.filer: Banks with domestic offices only and total consolidated assets of less than $100 billion that are not advanced-approaches institutions

FFIEC 051.name: Consolidated Reports of Condition and Income (streamlined report for small domestic banks)
FFIEC 051.filer: Banks with domestic offices only and total assets of less than $5 billion (excluding advanced-approaches/Category III and large/highly-complex assessment filers)

schedules.title: Charge-offs and Recoveries on Loans and Leases
Changes in Allowances for Credit Losses

FFIEC 002.name: Report of Assets and Liabilities of U.S. Branches and Agencies of Foreign Banks
FFIEC 002.filer: All US branches and agencies of foreign (and Puerto Rican) banks, federally or state licensed, insured or uninsured, including their IBFs

schedules.title: Cash and Balances Due from Depository Institutions
Due from / Due to Related Institutions in the U.S. and in Foreign Countries
Past Due, Nonaccrual, and Restructured Loans
Other Data for Deposit Insurance Assessments

FFIEC 002S.name: Report of Assets and Liabilities of a Non-U.S. Branch Managed or Controlled by a U.S. Branch or Agency of a Foreign Bank
FFIEC 002S.filer: Any US branch/agency filing the FFIEC 002 that manages or controls one or more non-US branches of its parent bank

schedules.title: Report of a Non-U.S. Branch Managed or Controlled by a U.S. Branch or Agency

FFIEC 009.name: Country Exposure Report
FFIEC 009.filer: US commercial banks, savings associations, bank/SLHC and IHCs with $30 million or more in total claims on residents of foreign countries

schedules.title: Claims by country (immediate-counterparty basis with redistribution to ultimate-risk basis)
Foreign-Office Liabilities by Country of Foreign Office and by Country of Creditor

FFIEC 009a.name: Country Exposure Information Report
FFIEC 009a.filer: Same filers as FFIEC 009 with material foreign-country exposures (over 1% of assets or 20% of capital)

schedules.title: Country exposures meeting the disclosure threshold

FFIEC 101.name: Regulatory Capital Reporting for Institutions Subject to the Advanced Capital Adequacy Framework
FFIEC 101.filer: Banks, savings associations, BHCs and SLHCs subject to the Advanced Capital Adequacy Framework (generally >= $250B assets or >= $10B foreign exposure) plus their depository subsidiaries

schedules.title: Wholesale Exposures - High Volatility Commercial Real Estate (HVCRE)

Retail Exposures - Residential Mortgage - Closed-end Junior Lien

FFIEC 102.name: Market Risk Regulatory Report for Institutions Subject to the Market Risk Capital Rule
FFIEC 102.filer: Banks/BHCs/SLHCs subject to the market-risk capital rule (generally trading assets+liabilities >= $1B or >= 10% of total assets)

schedules.title: Standardized Measurement Method / De Minimis Positions

FR Y-7.name: Annual Report of Foreign Banking Organizations
FR Y-7.filer: Each FBO engaged in banking in the US through a subsidiary bank, Edge/agreement corporation, commercial-lending company, or its own branches/agencies

FR Y-7N.name: Financial Statements of U.S. Nonbank Subsidiaries Held by Foreign Banking Organizations
FR Y-7N.filer: FBOs, per US nonbank subsidiary, by asset-size thresholds

schedules.title: Changes in Allowance for Credit Losses

FR Y-7NS.name: Abbreviated Financial Statements of U.S. Nonbank Subsidiaries Held by Foreign Banking Organizations
FR Y-7NS.filer: FBOs with US nonbank subsidiaries of assets $250M-$500M

FR Y-7Q.name: Capital and Asset Report for Foreign Banking Organizations
FR Y-7Q.filer: All FBOs with US banking operations

FR Y-10.name: Report of Changes in Organizational Structure
FR Y-10.filer: Top-tier BHCs/FHCs, top-tier SLHCs, state member banks not controlled by a BHC/FBO, Edge and agreement corporations, FBOs, and SHCs

schedules.title: Foreign Branches of U.S. Banking Organizations

FR Y-11.name: Financial Statements of U.S. Nonbank Subsidiaries of U.S. Holding Companies
FR Y-11.filer: Top-tier holding companies, per nonbank subsidiary, by thresholds

schedules.title: Changes in Allowance for Credit Losses

FR Y-11S.name: Abbreviated Financial Statements of U.S. Nonbank Subsidiaries of U.S. Holding Companies
FR Y-11S.filer: Top-tier holding companies with nonbank subsidiaries of assets $250M-$500M below quarterly criteria

FR Y-12.name: Consolidated Holding Company Report of Equity Investments in Nonfinancial Companies
FR Y-12.filer: Top-tier domestic holding companies that file the FR Y-9C or FR Y-9SP and meet the investment-size thresholds

schedules.title: Type of Entity within the Banking Organization
Nonfinancial Investment Transactions During the Reporting Period

FR Y-12A.name: Annual Report of Merchant Banking Investments Held for an Extended Period
FR Y-12A.filer: Financial holding companies holding nonfinancial merchant-banking investments beyond the routine holding period

FR Y-6.name: Annual Report of Holding Companies
FR Y-6.filer: All top-tier holding companies (BHCs, SLHCs, SHCs; certain ESOPs; non-QFBO foreign organizations)

FR Y-8.name: The Bank Holding Company Report of Insured Depository Institutions' Section 23A Transactions
FR Y-8.filer: All top-tier US BHCs (incl. FHCs and IHCs), all SLHCs, and all FBOs that directly own a US subsidiary bank

FR Y-14A.name: Capital Assessments and Stress Testing Report - Annual
FR Y-14A.filer: US BHCs, IHCs of foreign banking organizations, and covered SLHCs with total consolidated assets of $100 billion or more

FR Y-14Q.name: Capital Assessments and Stress Testing Report - Quarterly
FR Y-14Q.filer: US BHCs, IHCs, covered SLHCs with $100 billion or more in total consolidated assets

FR Y-14M.name: Capital Assessments and Stress Testing Report - Monthly
FR Y-14M.filer: US BHCs, IHCs, covered SLHCs with $100 billion or more in total consolidated assets that hold the relevant portfolios

schedules.title: Domestic First Lien Closed-end 1-4 Family Residential Loan
Domestic Home Equity Loan and Home Equity Line

FR Y-15.name: Banking Organization Systemic Risk Report
FR Y-15.filer: US BHCs and covered SLHCs >= $100B; FBOs with combined US assets >= $100B; and US firms designated as G-SIBs

FR 2052a.name: Complex Institution Liquidity Monitoring Report
FR 2052a.filer: Large banking organizations subject to LCR/NSFR - primarily Category I-III firms

FR 2900.name: Report of Transaction Accounts, Other Deposits, and Vault Cash
FR 2900.filer: Depository institutions (commercial banks, savings institutions, credit unions, US branches/agencies of foreign banks) under Regulation D

Pillar 3.name: Basel III Public Disclosure Templates and Tables
Pillar 3.regulator: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BIS); US disclosures via FFIEC 101/102 and the market-risk rule
Pillar 3.filer: Internationally large/advanced banks; in the US, advanced-approaches and market-risk-rule institutions

FDIC SDI.name: Statistics on Depository Institutions
FDIC SDI.filer: All FDIC-insured commercial banks and savings institutions (sourced from their Call Reports)

FDIC SOD.name: Summary of Deposits
FDIC SOD.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (jointly with OCC for the survey)
FDIC SOD.filer: All FDIC-insured institutions with branch offices, including insured US branches of foreign banks

FDIC Failures.name: Failures and Assistance Transactions (Research Information System / RIS)
FDIC Failures.filer: Failed and FDIC-assisted institutions (records back to the 1930s)

NCUA 5300.name: NCUA 5300 Call Report (Credit Unions) and Profile (FS220A)

schedules.title: Schedule H - Complex Credit-Union Leverage Ratio

OCC Historical.name: OCC Historical National Bank Reports (Annual Report of the Comptroller of the Currency)
OCC Historical.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
OCC Historical.filer: National banks (federally chartered commercial banks) via periodic Reports of Condition, 1863-1941

UBPR.name: Uniform Bank Performance Report
UBPR.filer: All banks - items are computed/derived from each bank's Call Report (FFIEC 031/041/051), not directly filed

NIC/NPW.name: National Information Center / NPW Institutional-Structure Bulk Data
NIC/NPW.filer: All entities the Fed tracks: banks, BHCs/FHCs, branches, savings institutions, holding-company tiers, some foreign and non-bank entities

SEC EDGAR.name: SEC EDGAR (bank holding company filings)
SEC EDGAR.filer: Public bank holding companies and other registrants (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, DEF 14A, XBRL)

HMDA.name: Home Mortgage Disclosure Act Loan/Application Register (LAR)
HMDA.filer: Covered depository institutions (banks, savings associations, credit unions) and non-depository mortgage lenders meeting coverage tests
HMDA.primary_identifier: LEI (2018-forward; pre-2018 Agency Code + Respondent ID)

MDRM.name: Micro Data Reference Manual
MDRM.filer: Meta-dictionary - defines the coded variables used across (nearly) every Fed/FFIEC reporting form
